messages.content:
I can’t run tests from here. I can, however, tell you exactly how to run “load test task 5” yourself — and what output to paste back so I can interpret results.

Brief reasoning: I don’t have access to your environment or repo, so you need to run the test locally/CI and share the logs or results.

What to do (pick the part that matches your project):

1) Find the task/test file
- Quick search: in your repo run:
  - grep -R "task 5" -n . 
  - or rg "task 5" -n .
  - or grep -R "task5" -n .
This tells you the test script or runner entry name.

2) Common runners — run commands
- npm / node script:
  - npm install
  - npm run <script-name>    (if script is task5: npm run task5)
  - or: node scripts/task5.js
- k6:
  - k6 run scripts/task5.js
  - for headless: k6 run --vus 100 --duration 1m scripts/task5.js
- Locust:
  - pip install -r requirements.txt (or pip install locust)
  - locust -f locustfile.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 --run-time 1m --only-summary
  - use tags if task5 mapped to a tag: --tags task5
- Artillery:
  - npm install -g artillery
  - artillery run task5.yml
- Gatling (Scala/Java):
  - mvn -Dgatling.simulationClass=com.example.Task5Simulation gatling:test
  - or sbt "gatling:testOnly com.example.Task5Simulation"
- Python pytest-based load tests:
  - pytest tests/load/test_task5.py -q
  - pytest -k task5
- Docker:
  - docker build -t loadtest .
  - docker run --rm loadtest ./run-task5.sh

3) Capture useful output
- Show peak RPS/requests, errors, response-time percentiles (p50/p95/p99), failure traces, CPU/memory on target if available, and the command you ran.
- If output is long, paste the summary and any error sections.

4) If you want an automated run in CI
- Tell me your CI (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins). I can give you a sample job snippet once you tell me the runner/tool.

If you want, paste:
- the test runner/tool name (k6/locust/artillery/whatever),
- the command you want to run (or the script path),
- and I’ll give the exact command and recommended flags to run “task 5” and how to parse the results.

If you want me to run it on your repo, submit again with a concrete coding/CI file request or turn off Chat-only mode.

nextActions.label: Continue chat
nextActions.description: Ask a follow-up question in the same thread.
